stemHtml: Before flight, the Commander notices a document from the maintenance organisation, the CRS (Certificate of Release to Service), which states that "all maintenance checks have been performed. Next check/valid until 5432 hours and 3254 landings." What does this mean?

options.a: The maintenance organisation has guaranteed the aircraft will be airworthy until the next check.
options.b: All maintenance checks (both hard-time and on-condition) have been completed and are valid until the limits in the CRS.
options.c: The CRS does not relate to airworthiness.
options.d: Only on-condition maintenance will be conducted at the next check.
